summary: Verified sidebar sticky-positioning fix in /app/frontend/src/pages/portal/PortalLayout.jsx. Logged in as admin and client, tested 10 long-scroll portal pages at desktop 1440x900, and mobile regression at 375x812. The sidebar now correctly stays pinned at viewport top=0 with the full dark navy (#0a2350) background always covering the left edge — the reported white/slate-50 gap below the 'Intercloud Admin' card is fully resolved.

left_edge_pixel_check_admin_products_scr.note: Bottom-left of viewport IS inside <aside>. Confirms no slate-50 gap.

test_evidence.header_and_ctas: Sticky main-content header (z-30 white bar with 'Signed in as ...') and top-right 'New Product' CTA remain visible and clickable at all scroll positions on admin/products (visible in screenshot).

critical_code_review_comments: PortalLayout.jsx line 113 fix is correct and minimal: `fixed lg:sticky top-0 left-0 h-screen lg:self-start w-72 ... flex flex-col flex-shrink-0`. Sticky+self-start pins the aside to the viewport top inside the flex parent while main content scrolls. No other file touched. No side-effects observed.

Minor (pre-existing, out of scope): the mobile hamburger button in the header uses no data-testid — recommend adding `data-testid="mobile-menu-toggle"` for easier automation; had to select via `header button:first`. Not blocking.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The sidebar sticky fix (lg:sticky top-0 h-screen lg:self-start flex-shrink-0) is verified across all 10 long-scroll portal pages (6 admin + 4 client) plus mobile regression. Bug from iteration screenshots is fully resolved. No regressions detected — main content header, CTA buttons, mobile hamburger, backdrop close, and X-button close all still work. If revisiting layout, note that <aside> has z-50 and mobile backdrop has z-40 (inset-0), so Playwright's default center-click on backdrop lands behind the open sidebar — use page.mouse.click(x>288, y) or click on the X button for reliable close.
